old_value: 3/30/26: Topeka submitted data for their second pass and we are expecting to have pass 2 loaded this week following several data corrections made by the client. Topeka is pushing out their HRM Go Live to June and are not on track to go live in EP&L in June so looking to push EP&L to September. I can make this work, but it will be very thin. In addition, they have hired another client, PM for the project. This will be the fourth PM on the project in addition to Zac. The client has shown some progress in the business tracker and with the new Go Live dates should be able to complete all pending tasks by that target. Zac has added additional calls with staff to keep things moving along and They seem to be getting a bit more focused which is good. Code is still very far behind and now reporting they are unsure if EP&L will meet their needs. They also still have one SME working on the configuration and testing who is expected to go on live in the coming months. I have addressed this with them as a critical risk. I will be interested to hear the feedback from staff on the data pass. | YELLOW 3/30/26: Client relationship remains good however Topeka continues to have internal staffing changes. A new Sr Project Manager was hired last month and is working with Zac on the Tyler Projects. Patti Biggs is the acting CIO, and Zac Shields, continues to be the main point of contact for the day-to-day management of the Tyler projects for Topeka. They are delayed on all implementations and moving HRM to June and EP&L to Sept. In the last few weeks, they have started to make process in testing so I am hopeful with the new Go Live date they can complete the pending tasks. This does extend things for several months.

old_value: 9/3/25: Cutler Bay is ready to move forward, but developing the plan is because Diamond Bar, CA, who have pushed their go-live, are still holding the six week window between 10/6 and 11/14. We're trying to schedule on-the-fly until Nate Venancio's availability in that window is confirmed. 8/6/25: Tyler Financials PM emailed client on 8/5/25 to state that both FIN and EPL implementations are until client signs off on chart of accounts. Kickoff July 15-17. | 9/3/25: A new ComDev director has been hired and the tone of status calls has improved. 8/6/25: Jared Munster, former ComDev Director, has left that position and has been hired by site as a consultant. JM is still listed as CDD on the CB website. Still trying to understand what this means for our implementation.  PM-to-PM call held on 6/5/25. Teckpert rep Adrian Esquivel stated that the project needs to come in under budget. Com Dev Dir Jared Munster is angry that staff have to build the config. Says he was not told that before EPL was selected.

old_value: 2/04/25: Project is due to an unknown GL date. The decision point is with the client’s stakeholders to determine GL change. St Petersburg steering committee meeting on 2/5/26 to determine the new requested date. Once this decision is made, this project will be shift back to .  The client is currently in the Validation stage, and we will begin Conversion, Report Development, and integrations in January.  Avolve Interface: Decided to go with ProjectDox and is looking to amend the contract so the the SOW is between Tyler and Avolve.

old_value: The client recently asked to break project into two phases. They want to go live with Parking Permits, Rental Inspections, and Fire in November 2025. Conversion is not included in their November go live. The remainder of their processes will go live on 4/20/2026 and a post production conversion will be required. Pending CO for 25 out-of-scope processes. | DigEPlan is a sore point for them. The way Tyler sold DigE Plan (too early) resulted in the client going directly to Avolve to purchase. TTyler contract lacks hours needed to support DigEPlan imp.  Client is reluctant to sign change orders.
